# crates/attractor-dot/src

Rust library providing DOT dialect parsing for Attractor pipelines. Parses a strict `digraph` Graphviz subset into typed AST nodes with support for duration, attribute, and subgraph constructs.

## Contents

[ast.rs](./ast.rs) — Defines `DotGraph`, `NodeDef`, `EdgeDef`, `SubgraphDef` root/record types and `AttributeValue` enum (String, Integer, Float, Boolean, Duration)

[duration_serde.rs](./duration_serde.rs) — Custom Serde for `std::time::Duration` ↔ human-readable strings ("20m", "500ms") with unit suffixes (ms, s, m, h, d)

[lib.rs](./lib.rs) — Public API barrel: re-exports AST types, `parse()` entry point, `duration_serde::parse_duration_str`

[parser.rs](./parser.rs) — winnow-based recursive descent parser converting input → `DotGraph`. Handles statements, edge chains, attr blocks, node/edge defaults, comment stripping, line/col error reporting

## Architecture

```
input string
    │
    ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  parser.rs          │  strip_comments → parse_digraph → statements → merge_statements
│  (winnow)           │  Returns DotGraph { name, attrs, nodes, edges, subgraphs, node_defaults, edge_defaults }
└─────────────────────┘
    │
    ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│  ast.rs             │  Typed AST structs with serde derives
│  duration_serde.rs  │  Duration ⇄ "Xms" string conversion
└─────────────────────┘
```

## Stack

- **Language**: Rust
- **Dependencies**: `serde` (Serialize/Deserialize traits), `winnow` (parser combinators), `attractor-types` (Duration type)
- **Entry point**: `parse(input: &str) -> Result<DotGraph, AttractorError>` from lib.rs

## Patterns

- **Barrel re-export**: lib.rs re-exports `ast::*`, `parser::parse`, `duration_serde::parse_duration_str`
- **Module visibility**: parser is `pub(crate)` — internal only; public API via lib.rs
- **Winnow parser combinators**: `ws`, `identifier`, `alt`, `map`, `fold` for recursive descent

## Behavioral Contracts

- Identifier regex: `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*`
- Duration suffixes: `ms`, `s`, `m`, `h`, `d`
- Rejects undirected graphs (`graph { ... }`) with `"'digraph' keyword (undirected graphs are not supported)"`
- Rejects undirected edges (`--`) with `"only directed edges (->); undirected edges (--) are not supported"`
- Rejects `strict` keyword with `"'digraph' keyword (strict graphs are not supported)"`
